method.baseline_success: mock-world executable success for the complete predicted invocation sequence
method.catalog_position: zero-based position in the displayed, task-keyed SHA-256 permutation returned by action_catalog
method.enum_position: zero-based position of each gold argument value in its public enum
method.overlap: exact and normalized hashes plus normalized-trace lexical bigram Jaccard at the pre-existing 0.80 flag threshold
method.catalog_position_goodness_of_fit: post-hoc descriptive chi-square against the task-size-conditional uniform expectation; not a preregistered gate and not used for filtering

basis: The stored final static audit deterministically recomputes to pass_unsealed and every declared hard check passes.
The v3 preregistration and annotation protocol require leakage and overlap measurement but do not specify a numeric rejection threshold for the 200-task confirmatory pool.

The final-audit policy explicitly treats shallow-probe success, normalized topology overlap, internal action-graph reuse, and lexical flags as reported diagnostics rather than automatic rejection rules.
There are no candidate-development family, entity-pool, generator-seed, exact-trace, normalized-trace, or exact-action-graph collisions and no lexical pair reaches Jaccard 0.80.
Exact-copy succeeds on all 200 tasks, while every evaluator-blind static hard gate passes.
decision.no_posthoc_threshold_statement: No new leakage, position-balance, or normalized-topology cutoff was invented after inspecting these diagnostics. The tasks must not be filtered using these observed results. A stricter cutoff would require a prospective versioned amendment and a new audit chain before model calls.
decision.qualification: PASS means compliant with the existing pre-seal contract, not absence of interface regularity or proof of structural independence.

confirmatory_v3_consistency_check.result: satisfies the development thresholds if used only as a nonbinding consistency reference

existing_threshold_context.interpretation: These development-test thresholds were not preregistered as confirmatory-v3 rejection thresholds and are not retroactively promoted here. No existing numeric threshold applies to the oracle-sequence plus enum-first diagnostic or normalized-topology flags.

name_only_renaming.definition: The probe receives the evaluator-only ordered oracle action-name sequence, including its length, and guesses each argument as enum[0], false, or unknown.
name_only_renaming.diagnostic_meaning: It is an upper-bound diagnostic for the combination of oracle sequence exposure and canonical argument ordering. It is not a public action-name-only learner and cannot show that public action names alone solve 30.5% of tasks.
name_only_renaming.main_risk: The excess over uniform guessing is concentrated in two-step paths, for which the oracle action sequence already reveals the workflow path and only arguments remain to be guessed.

first_action_enum_fields.interpretation: The first action is materially skewed toward enum index 0, commonly for clarification or authorization arguments.

terminal_primary_argument_by_task.interpretation: Terminal primary choices are approximately balanced, but whole-path enum-first success remains possible because earlier action arguments are not similarly balanced.

terminal_gold_action_position.interpretation: This marginal post-hoc deviation is reported, not used as a gate; it was not preregistered and is one of several position summaries.

catalog_position.implementation_caveat: The catalog_only baseline canonicalizes catalog content by sorting it and is intentionally invariant to displayed transport order. Therefore it does not by itself measure displayed-position leakage. The final hard check called catalog_permutation_safe verifies deterministic reconstruction, public-name coverage, uniqueness, and evaluator-key absence; its self-recomputation equality is not a statistical balance test. The explicit position summaries above provide the separate diagnostic.

candidate_vs_development.interpretation: Normalization erases action names, keys, and concrete values, so a collision can reflect a common abstract ask/authorize/commit skeleton. It is not proof of semantic duplication, but the concentration means the release must not claim a structural holdout.

candidate_internal.interpretation: The pool has 200 unique family labels and trace identities, but not 200 unique action graphs. Claims should use the former wording and avoid asserting graph-level independence.

reporting_requirements: Report catalog-only 14/200, predicate-only 12/200, exact-copy 200/200, and the accurately renamed oracle-sequence plus enum-first diagnostic 61/200 (30.5%).
Report the domain and sequence-length concentration of shallow successes and normalized-topology flags.
State that normalized topology overlap is a diagnostic abstraction match, not semantic identity, and do not claim structural holdout.
Do not use research/action_name_leakage_audit_v2 or its 69/200 value as the confirmatory-v3 result; it describes the superseded candidate-v2 pool.
Update manuscript text that currently reports 69/200 and 34.5% before public release.
Do not filter or repair tasks using these observed diagnostics after this audit.
nonblocking_implementation_note: scripts/freeze_split.py binds the final-audit byte hash but does not itself assert the final-audit status or all hard_checks. The current bound audit independently recomputes as PASS; adding a fail-closed content assertion would strengthen future sealing safety without changing tasks or thresholds.
